description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-topped wooden bucket or barrel, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two prominent black metal hoops—one near the top rim and one near the base. The wood has a dark, weathered finish with visible grain and some subtle stains, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The interior is also wooden, matching the exterior in color and texture, and the rim is slightly thicker, providing structural reinforcement. There are no visible handles or lids, and the object is entirely open at the top. The metal bands are slightly textured and appear to be securely fastened, preventing the staves from separating. The overall shape is slightly tapered, wider at the top and narrowing toward the base, typical of traditional barrels or large buckets used for storage or carrying materials.
description: A weathered, open-topped wooden bucket or barrel with black metal hoops and a dark, aged finish. The object is slightly tapered and lacks handles or a lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, dark wood grain, and both metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Side view with similar features, highlighting the curvature and even spacing of the staves.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, with subtle wood grain variations.
description_view_3: Rear view, showing the same open top and metal bands, with minor wood stains visible.
